It says what if you could plug any book into your brain and instantly download that knowledge and information so you could implement it into your life. Do you want to learn a new language? Learn how to make more money? Get ready for a test? What if there's a way that you could learn it almost instantly and retain it all much longer? It reminds me of one of my favorite movies. Have you ever seen the movie The Matrix? Do you remember the scene where the main character Neo had a socket in the back of his skull that allowed him to instantly upload knowledge with no effort on his part? He was able to instantly learn Jiu Jitsu, Kung Fu, and other martial arts disciplines just by uploading the information to his brain. This allowed him to spar with Morpheus and impress his crew with his newfound skills. When he got into trouble, he was able to download a program that gave him the skills he needed to fly a helicopter to get out of danger. Just imagine if you had that ability. What would you learn? What information would you want if you could instantly download it into your brain? Now of course we have the ability to learn anything we want through books, the internet, and in school. But unfortunately, getting that knowledge isn't as easy as inserting a socket into our brains and just downloading the information. We have to find a good one or two hours to open a book, sit down to read, and even if we read fast, it's going to take days to get the information out of the book that we actually need. And then when you look at the internet to speed up the process, but quickly become bogged down with millions of pages for listings for any topic you want to study. Talk about information overload. Have you ever felt that frustration and I can relate to you perfectly? In school, I struggled to learn the information I needed to pass the test. In my business, I struggled with information overload and the need to keep up with the changes that are constantly happening around me. I was always frustrated because there were some things I wanted to learn for fun but couldn't find the time or the energy to sit and read for fun. That was until one day I had a chance meeting with a guy named Howard Burke. According to Gettys Book of Worlds Records, Howard is the world's fastest reader. He can read 30,000 words a minute while the average person can only read about 200 words a minute. Isn't that crazy? Check this out. I have a clip here of him doing a bunch of crazy readings things. This is after I met Howard. He spent just a few short hours with me. He was able to almost instantly double how fast I could read. I was so amazed and excited that I actually flew him to my office. We blocked out a few hours and we had him train my entire staff. Just look what happened when we finished. We had a video clip of him actually in my office and our staff everyone freaking out because they double or triple their reading. I started telling my friends and family members and business associates about what Howard did in my office. What happened next shocked me. Some people were begging me to meet with him so they could double their reading speed. Others were really skeptical because others were really skeptical that it was even possible. So you are the guru on the mountain that they want to become, but they want to know that you came, that you were where they were at. You put you at their level and point out your frustrations, which by the way, by the way are their frustrations. And that's a big goal. That's how you're getting them to like you. My camera was one of the famous copywriters back in the day said that, I'm sure I'm going to slaughter, but something about the point where if you can better, if you can explain the situation that that person, if you can better explain the situation someone's in than they can, then it doesn't matter if you offer the right solution or not. That's the fact you're able to better explain their situation. They're more likely to purchase from you. I wish I knew that quote on top of my head. But the basically is by me pointing out their frustrations, I'm trying to tap in the same frustrations they're already feeling. If I can do that, then boom, I've got it because now they came on the same path as me. They're frustrated just like me because I was able to explain their frustrations. It doesn't matter what I say next, they're going to be it because I was able to clearly explain the frustrations they were feeling. That quote was awesome. I wish I knew what it was. But that was basically the gist was if you can explain their frustrations they're having, then that's what they need, more so than they use selling anything else. If you can explain it clearly where they feel like you understand them, that's it. That's the goal. That's where that part comes from. Now this is where you introduce the magic bullet. It's very simple. I raise my questions. I get them focusing on imagery. I then introduce myself to bring me to their level. I point out my frustrations, which are also their frustrations. I offer my magic bullet. I explain how I discovered it and I associate my value. I tell them, don't worry. I point out the pink elephant in the room and I rebuild the price. Then I approve it to you. I'm going to do it for free, risk reversal. Then we close. Give them the two options and let them go and sign up. I guess, guys, it's a very simple, easy process. You're going to get this result by being on the webinar. If not, you don't pay anything. If you don't have a result like that yet, that's what becomes difficult. I was consulting two of my friends, Mike Filsey and Andy Jenkins. See, you guys may know them. They're two really big marketers here in the States. And this is the one, I do probably 90% of my videos this way because it's so simple and so much easier. Dagan's on his. If you look at this, he actually footage him at a seminar. Yes, he is not easy. The easiest way, and I think the best way is the simple PowerPoint style. Unless you feel really comfortable and you can do a headshot when you stand up and actually go through the script. But it's hard, at least for me, it's hard for you to keep on the script and make it sound like I'm not reading. Whereas the PowerPoint, I can do it a lot easier. Okay, now my voice is really going, guys. I really apologize. So the PowerPoint style is how I primarily do all of mine. I create a PowerPoint and then I open up Camtasia and I click record and I record myself walking through slide after slide after slide. And that's basically how I do it. Because it's simple and it's easy and if I make mistakes, it's pretty easy to edit those mistakes out as well inside of Camtasia. So that's kind of what I would recommend, especially for now. Powerpoint, especially to begin with, because if you want to change them, it's very easy to change on the PowerPoint. When we had our call center, maybe Laugh, we had our Pequate 60 sales guys, but every single week we'd add 10 sales guys and 10 would leave, so kind of like this constant rotation. And the ones that left that didn't make money were always the ones who tried to take the script and make it better, because they had always screwed up and the guys who just followed the script to the tee, they made the money, and it was just consistent like that.
